sourceGate.rule: published_at or updated_at must be earlier than kickoff_utc8 to enter this context

media.summary: England were favourites but under pressure; Croatia were described as resilient and difficult to face. Preview expected a close contest and leaned England win with under 3.5 goals.
Goal framed the game as a high-significance opener: England aggressive and organised under Tuchel, Croatia disciplined, pragmatic, and technically composed.
Oddschecker reported England's perfect defensive qualifying run and Croatia's strong qualifying campaign, then predicted England 2-0.

teamNews.summary: England expected Stones/Konsa at centre back, Bellingham ahead of Rogers, Saka fitness managed, Livramento withdrawn.
Croatia had fitness notes around Gvardiol, Modric, and Kovacic, but all were expected to be fit enough to start.
Confirmed pre-kickoff lineups had England starting Pickford, O'Reilly, Konsa, Stones, James, Anderson, Rice, Bellingham, Gordon, Kane and Madueke; Saka, Guehi and Rashford were reserves.
Croatia started Livakovic, Gvardiol, Sutalo, Vuskovic, Stanisic, Modric, Pasalic, Sucic, Perisic, Baturina and Musa.

formAndTactics.summary: England were reported as winning all eight qualifiers without conceding; Croatia were reported as seven wins and one draw in qualifying, conceding four.
Previews expected a close match rather than a free-scoring mismatch, partly because of Croatia experience and tournament resilience.

marketRead: Baseline low-score scores were all available: 0-0, 0-1, 1-0, 1-1.
1-1 had one of the lowest correct-score odds at 5.8, while 0-0 and 0-1 drifted upward from first odds.
FOX listed England -140, Croatia +420 and Draw +270; DraftKings listed England -145, Croatia +425 and Draw +275.
FOX and FanDuel-style market reads leaned toward under 2.5, but only FOX had an exact trusted timestamp in this sample.

weatherAndVenue.summary: Venue was Dallas Stadium / AT&T Stadium in Arlington, Texas.
Hot-weather context is audit-only because no exact pre-match timestamp was captured.
